rulings.Case Title: TONY FU VS. DEMAS YAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Bifurcate Kaman Lius Cross-Action And Merge It With Legal Recovery Action (Motions)

PLAINTIFF TONY FU Motion To Bifurcate Kaman Lius Cross-Action And Merge It With Legal Recovery Action (Motions) is CONTINUED to June 7, 2022 on the Court's own motion. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: TSUNGYUAN CHANG VS. ALAN WU KONG TAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Enforce Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 22, 2022 line 4. PLAINTIFF TSUNGYUAN CHANG, CHIU-LAN HUANG, MING LIN, SHU-JUNG CHEN, CHI-CHEN CHOU, MEI-HSING CHEN, RAKER YU, HUI-CHIN HUANG Motion To Enforce Settlement is GRANTED, in part. Plaintiffs are ordered to sign any and all necessary documents to effectuate the close of escrow. (Settlement Agreement, Section 20.) The payment obligations under the Settlement Agreement shall be made within 45 days following the close of escrow. Plaintiff's request for attorney's fees and costs is denied. Plaintiffs are not the "substantially prevailing party" on their motion to enforce the Settlement Agreement. By the time the instant motion was filed, the parties were ready, and have been ready, to close escrow. Plaintiffs are not entitled to prejudgment interest. Prejudgment interest under CC 3287(a) is only available when a plaintiff is entitled to recover damages. Plaintiff have not established that they were entitled to damages as a result of their partition action. (See Union Pacific Railroad Co. v. Santa Fe Pacific Pipelines, Inc. (2014) 231 Cal.App.4th 134, 200, 202.) =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 22, 2022 line 6. Defendant Gramercy Towers Condominium Association Gramercy Towers Condominium Association'S Joinder To Special Motion To Strike Plaintiff'S Second Amended Complaint Per C.C.P. Sec.425.16 (Anti-Slapp) By David Wetzel, Scott Gordon, Stephen Rogers, Jyotsna Bires, Giampaolo D'Errico, Joseph O. Tobin, Ii (tentative ruling purposes only Part 3 Of 3) 13. Ninth Cause of Action for Denial of Plaintiff's Rights to Attend Meetings. No authority is cited that the alleged conduct is an exercise of First Amendment rights pursuant to the anti-SLAPP statute. Therefore, the first prong of the anti-SLAPP analysis is not met. 14. Tenth Cause of Action for Unauthorized Transfer of Funds. No authority is cited that the alleged conduct is an exercise of First Amendment rights pursuant to the anti-SLAPP statute. Therefore, the first prong of the anti-SLAPP analysis is not met. 15. Eleventh Cause of Action for Refusal to Provide Requisite Insurance. No authority is cited that the alleged conduct is an exercise of First Amendment rights pursuant to the anti-SLAPP statute. Therefore, the first prong of the anti-SLAPP analysis is not met. (End of tentative ruling part 3 of 3) =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). rulings.Case Title: JOANNA L. PFEISTER VS. GRAMERCY TOWERS CONDOMINIUM ASSOCIATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Gramercy Towers Condominium Association Gramercy Towers Condominium Association'S Joinder To Special Motion To Strike Plaintiff'S Second Amended Complaint Per C.C.P. Sec.425.16 (Anti-Slapp) By David Wetzel, Scott Gordon, Stephen Rogers, Jyotsna Bires, Giampaolo D'Errico, Joseph O. Tobin, Ii (Tentative Ruling Purposes Only Part 2 Of 3)
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 22, 2022 line 6. Defendant Gramercy Towers Condominium Association Gramercy Towers Condominium Association'S Joinder To Special Motion To Strike Plaintiff'S Second Amended Complaint Per C.C.P. Sec.425.16 (Anti-Slapp) By David Wetzel, Scott Gordon, Stephen Rogers, Jyotsna Bires, Giampaolo D'Errico, Joseph O. Tobin, Ii (Tentative Ruling Purposes Only Part 2 Of 3) 7) Third Cause of Action for Malicious Prosecution. Malicious prosecution lies against private defendants who have sought out police authorities and falsely reported facts to them indicating that plaintiff has committed a crime. (Sullivan v. County of Los Angeles (1974) 12 Cal.App.3d 710, 720.) Plaintiff states in her declaration that Defendants knowingly made false statements to law enforcement officers and asked them to arrest plaintiff for the purposes of instigating a criminal action against plaintiff which was subsequently dismissed and for providing a pretext for a civil harassment action by defendants against plaintiff which was also later dismissed. Therefore, Plaintiff has produced sufficient evidence to show a prima facie case for Malicious Prosecution. 8) Fourth Cause of Action for Invasion of Privacy. No authority is cited showing that the general hiring/use of attorneys/private investigators is speech protected by anti-SLAPP statute. Therefore, the first prong of the anti-SLAPP analysis is not met. 9) Fifth Cause of Action for Elder Abuse. Much of the conduct alleged by Plaintiff in this Cause of Action is not speech for the purposes of the anti-SLAPP statute. Further, Plaintiff provides sufficient evidence in their declaration setting out a prima facie case that she has a cause of action against Defendants. 10. Sixth Cause of Action for Intentional Infliction of Emotional Distress. Plaintiff alleges intentional infliction of emotional distress resulting from defendants' actions to discourage and exclude her from service on the Board on account of her gender identity, and disability and veteran status, including violations of the Unruh Civil Rights Act and Ralph Act. P claims Defendants' actions were obviously calculated and intentional and taken without regard for the probability that their acts would cause plaintiff to suffer severe emotional distress. This is sufficient evidence to set out a prima facie showing on this claim. 11. Seventh Cause of Action for Failure to Conduct Annual Meetings. No authority is cited that the alleged conduct is an exercise of First Amendment rights pursuant to the anti-SLAPP statute. Therefore, the first prong of the anti-SLAPP analysis is not met. 12. Eighth Cause of Action for Denial of Right to Inspect Records. No authority is cited that the alleged conduct is an exercise of First Amendment rights pursuant to the anti-SLAPP statute. Therefore, the first prong of the anti-SLAPP analysis is not met. (End of tenative ruling part 2 of 2) =(501/CFH)

rulings.Case Title: JOANNA L. PFEISTER VS. GRAMERCY TOWERS CONDOMINIUM ASSOCIATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Gramercy Towers Condominium Association'S Joinder To Special Motion To Strike Plaintiff'S Second Amended Complaint Per C.C.P. Sec.425.16 (Anti-Slapp) By David Wetzel, Scott Gordon, Stephen Rogers, Jyotsna Bires, Giampaolo D'Errico, Joseph O. Tobin, Ii
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 22, 2022 line 6. DEFENDANT GRAMERCY TOWERS CONDOMINIUM ASSOCIATION Gramercy Towers Condominium Association'S Joinder To Special Motion To Strike Plaintiff'S Second Amended Complaint Per C.C.P. Sec.425.16 (Anti-Slapp) By David Wetzel, Scott Gordon, Stephen Rogers, Jyotsna Bires, Giampaolo D'Errico, Joseph O. Tobin, Ii (Tentative ruling part 1 of 3) 1) Plaintiff served the FAC on Defendants between November 23 and November 26, 2021. The SAC, filed February 7, 2022, only alleged one new cause of action not included in the FAC (violation of privacy). This anti-SLAPP motion was filed on March 8, 2022, more than 60 days after the FAC was served on Defendants. The Motion is untimely as to the claims alleged in the FAC. The only claim not untimely is COA Four for invasion of privacy. 2) The anti-SLAPP motion is denied on Invasion of Privacy, as Plaintiff demonstrates a probability of prevailing on the claim. 3) Defendant Gramercy Tower's motion to join lacks sufficient notice, and in addition it does not request affirmative relief. 4) On the merits, Defendant Board of Director's Special Motion to Strike Plaintiff's Second Amended Complaint per CCP Sec. 425.16 is DENIED. For each claim, Plaintiff demonstrates a probability of prevailing, or the claim is based on conduct not protected by the anti-SLAPP statute. 5) First Cause of Action for Discrimination. Plaintiff's declaration constitutes a prima facie showing of discrimination, particularly as she claims she is a member of protected classes and that she was subjected to disparate, adverse treatment through a direct and circumstantial showing. (See, e.g., Guz v. Bechtel National, Inc. (2000) 24 Cal.4th 317, 355). Further, not all conduct alleged in this cause of action is speech for the purposes of the anti-SLAPP statute. 6) Second Cause of Action for Abuse of Process. Plaintiff claims Defendant made false statements to law enforcement officers about plaintiff's conduct during the settlement conference for the purposes of instigating a wrongful criminal prosecution against plaintiff (which was dismissed) and to create a false premise to support a civil case by defendants to obtain a restraining order against plaintiff (which was also later dismissed) in order to further defendants' plan to exclude plaintiff from serving on the Board on account of her protected status. Plaintiff provides sufficient evidence to establish a prima facie case for Abuse of Process. Further, not all conduct alleged in this cause of action is speech for the purposes of the anti-SLAPP statute. (End of tentative ruling Part 1 of 3) =(501/CFH)

(See, e.g., Guz v. Bechtel National, Inc. (2000) 24 Cal.4th 317, 355). Therefore, Plaintiff has produced sufficient evidence to show a prima facie case for Malicious Prosecution. (End of tentative ruling Part 1 of 3) =(501/CFH)

rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT To The First-Amended Complaint Of Pamela Uberti, Or, In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 22, 2022 line 7. DEFENDANT GALE LEA GRINSELL,, FOUNDERS REALTY, INC. MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT To The First-Amended Complaint Of Pamela Uberti, Or, In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication is CONTINUED to May 16, 2022 for Defendant to comply with SFRL 2.7B and provide courtesy copies of the moving papers no later than 10:00 a.m. on April 25, 2022 with a cover letter reflecting hew hearing date. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: STRUCTURE PROPERTIES, INC. VS. NICHOLAS CHRISTIE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant'S Attendance At His Deposition, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant ; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for April 22, 2022 line 12. PLAINTIFF STRUCTURE PROPERTIES, INC. Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendant'S Attendance At His Deposition, And Request For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant is GRANTED. Defendant is ordered to sit for deposition on or before May 6, 2022. The parties are ordered to meet and confer on an agreed upon date, time, and location. Sanctions are granted in the amount of $1,800. Plaintiff will reasonably accommodate Defendant in the following ways: The deposition will be limited to 4 hours on the first day. If more time is needed, the deposition will be continued to another day, but in no event, later than May 6, 2022; Only one Plaintiff's attorney will be in the room; There will be one five-minute break every hour. =(501/CFH) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303; or Phone Dial in: (415) 762-9988; Webinar ID: 836 2280 0051; Password: 613303). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.
